rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless): 2 pass 0 fail — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. Real run captured in headless Chrome (fresh profile): first visit showed Download (~290 MB), after download the model produced REAL output for 'Roses are red, violets are [BLANK].' -> 'blue and brown ..beige, violets inclie.' in 0.66 s on WASM (59 byte/s, 30 input bytes). No pipeline() is used — the byte-level model is driven directly via T5ForConditionalGeneration with a hand-applied byte tokenizer, because ByT5 has no tokenizer.json and Transformers.js ships no ByT5Tokenizer.
rubric.notes: Base 300M byte-span-denoising checkpoint — completions are genuinely rough (contains 'blue', the correct rhyme, plus loose continuation). Shown honestly, never canned.

rubric.evidence: conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual. Verified by agent: output is a decoded byte span (UTF-8) that references the prompt; the fill is highlighted in the completed sentence. Output byte ids stay in 3..257 (text bytes); sentinels 258+ delimit the fill. Real sample above.
rubric.notes: A rare stray replacement char can appear when the model emits an incomplete multi-byte UTF-8 sequence at a span boundary — honest byte-level behaviour, not a render bug.

rubric.evidence: conformance: 5 pass — declares-model-id (onnx-community/byt5-small-ONNX, linked), declares-task (text2text-generation), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker) all pass. At-a-glance also documents the 384-id byte 'vocabulary' honestly.

conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual, VERIFIED: a second page load in the same profile (cache present) auto-initialised to state=ready with status 'Model ready — running locally on your device.' and NO Download button (hasDownloadButton=false), 0 console errors. First visit correctly showed the Download control (download-required).

conformance: 3 pass (live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, runs-without-webgpu-fallback) + honest-progress-retry-offline=manual. Download progress renders in the shared loader progress bar; failures surface as an .err status with a Retry path (createModelLoader). WASM model needs no WebGPU — runs on any device.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; retry/offline paths inherited from the shared loader.

rubric.evidence: conformance: controls-operable=manual. Verified controls: preset buttons fill the textarea; max-new-bytes + beams range sliders with live value labels; Insert [BLANK] button; Generate; the live 'see inside' byte input updates chips + stats on every keystroke (main thread, no model). All are real button/input elements with visible labels and focus-visible outlines.
rubric.notes: Manual-evidenced controls confirmed operable in the headless run (preset click + Generate click drove a real generation).

rubric.evidence: conformance: no-fake-output=manual. Confirmed: the only output shown is decoded from the model's real generate() ids; there is no hardcoded fallback string. Empty output renders '(empty output)'/blank marker. The byte 'see inside' is computed live from TextEncoder, not stored.

conformance: 2 pass — images-have-alt=pass (the page uses no img element; the icon is an inline SVG data URI), buttons-have-names=pass. Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; every control has an associated label.

conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280x800) clean, mobile (360x740 DPR3) clean — no horizontal overflow on either class. Agent read screenshots: byte chips wrap with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 (no overflow from long id runs); controls stack on mobile. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/byt5-byte-level/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/byt5-byte-level/mobile.png
rubric.notes: Light + dark both verified via emulated prefers-color-scheme (warm paper / dark), both legible.

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs in a Web Worker (byt5.js ByT5Engine -> worker.js), so the control UI and the live byte 'see inside' stay responsive during generation. The byte-chip render is capped (limit) to avoid a long main-thread task on huge pastes.
rubric.notes: Applied identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks: off-main-thread inference + bounded DOM work.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: For 1D item rows use flexbox with flex-wrap; set min-inline-size:0 on flex items containing long unbreakable content; use repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) for responsive card/stat grids.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the byte-chip strip is flex-flow:row wrap with min-inline-size:0 on the container column so long token-id runs never force horizontal overflow on mobile; preset and stat rows use auto-fit minmax grids.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy JS off the main thread and keep interaction handlers short to protect INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model download + generate run in a Web Worker; the live byte visualisation is O(chars) and capped so keystrokes stay cheap.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up / bound long main-thread loops so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — renderByteChips caps the number of chips (limit) so a huge paste can't build an unbounded DOM in one task.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from public/styles.css @view-transition navigation:auto (invariant 6); the use-case cards use the shared .model-card content-visibility grid.

openQuestions: Would a beam-search or sampling default noticeably improve the base model's rough fills, or is greedy the most honest default at this size?
Should the demo add a fine-tuned ByT5 (e.g. a spelling-correction or transliteration head) as a NEW slug to show the robustness payoff on a task the base model can't do well?
Is the occasional replacement char at a span boundary worth trimming (drop a trailing incomplete UTF-8 sequence) or is showing it more honest about byte-level generation?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for byt5-byte-level: Read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm tap targets + focus order + light/dark contrast, then flip support to ok.

Add a NEW demo for a fine-tuned byte-level model (spelling correction / transliteration / grapheme-to-phoneme with a real browser ONNX) to show ByT5's noise-robustness on a task the base checkpoint can't do — additive slug, never replacing this page.
Re-check InternVL (blocked): if Transformers.js adds an InternVL model class + processor and a canonical onnx-community ONNX export ships, build the on-device VLM demo.

summary: Revision-2 build critique for byt5-byte-level (text2text-generation, WASM q8), evidenced by a real headless-browser run (first-visit download -> real 0.66 s output; cached reload auto-inits with no button, 0 console errors) plus the conformance run (14/14 auto-checkable pass, 9 manual-evidenced) and the responsive matrix (desktop+mobile clean, light+dark verified). The star is the token-free byte tokenizer (no vocabulary, 0 unknown tokens for any script/emoji), shown live. The base model's fills are honestly rough and never canned.
